Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 31 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. My first patch to the Linux kernel 156 upvotes, 29 comments
  3. Apple's intentional crippling of Mobile Safari continues 21 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. A Review of Dice That Came with the White Castle 36 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 53 upvotes, 8 comments
  6. Some things just take time 773 upvotes, 249 comments
  7. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 232 upvotes, 77 comments
  8. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 316 upvotes, 114 comments
  9. Bored of eating your own dogfood? Try smelling your own farts 126 upvotes, 41 comments
  10. 25 Years of Eggs 131 upvotes, 43 comments
  11. HopTab–free,open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 72 upvotes, 21 comments
  12. Flash-Moe: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Mac with 48GB RAM 145 upvotes, 51 comments
  13. 'Miracle': Europe reconnects with lost spacecraft 74 upvotes, 29 comments
  14. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Turns out your coffee addiction may be doing your brain a favor 27 upvotes, 8 comments
  16. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 539 upvotes, 309 comments
  17. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 393 upvotes, 229 comments
  18. Chest Fridge (2009) 150 upvotes, 79 comments
  19. Brute-Forcing My Algorithmic Ignorance with an LLM in 7 Days 29 upvotes, 10 comments
  20. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 31 upvotes, 12 comments
  21. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 38 upvotes, 16 comments
  22. Why Lab Coats Turned White 13 upvotes, 3 comments
  23. Hormuz Minesweeper – Are you tired of winning? 464 upvotes, 317 comments
  24. Vatican Rebukes Peter Thiel's Antichrist Lectures in Rome 102 upvotes, 71 comments
  25. Convincing Is Not Persuading 16 upvotes, 9 comments
  26. Revise – An AI Editor for Documents 18 upvotes, 13 comments
  27. A Case Against Currying 37 upvotes, 37 comments
  28. $ teebot.dev – from terminal to tee in 6 seconds 15 upvotes, 12 comments
  29. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 10 upvotes, 8 comments
  30. Show HN: Crack – Turn your MacBook into a squeaky door 8 upvotes, 8 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 22 March 2026. Source here.
